{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016qr7cAahiGgTbynJJJmJC6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"ff38f287-1a7d-40b4-ba4d-ccec95b7943f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:00:08.435Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DpEuxNTPYSrH6M9hYA35jj","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (smart: skips rebuild when no new commits; `--force` to override)\n    46→- Deploy script compares HEAD before/after `git pull` — use `--force` when code was already pulled but not yet built\n    47→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    48→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    49→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    50→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    51→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    52→\n    53→## React + SQLite\n    54→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    55→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    56→\n    57→## Security\n    58→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    59→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    60→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    61→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    62→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    63→\n    64→## Working Directory\n    65→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    66→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    67→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    68→\n    69→## Security\n    70→- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n    71→- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n    72→- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n    73→- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n    74→- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n    75→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"9d0d140a-c3ca-4959-ab70-3e0d8ef03809","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:00:08.914Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (smart: skips rebuild when no new commits; `--force` to override)\n- Deploy script compares HEAD before/after `git pull` — use `--force` when code was already pulled but not yet built\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n\n## Security\n- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n","numLines":75,"startLine":1,"totalLines":75}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NMszqWozCtrbqjxHFpwoTv","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 12:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 15: found and fixed 6 bugs across search injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, and transporter caching. Created issue #104, implemented fixes in PR #105, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    12→\n    13→## Work Completed\n    14→\n    15→### Issue #104: Code review round 15 (PR #105 — merged + deployed)\n    16→\n    17→6 fixes:\n    18→\n    19→1. **`auto-discord` uses `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()`** (IMPORTANT) — Bypassed DB user-existence check that all other authenticated endpoints perform. Deleted users with valid cookies could still access this endpoint. Fixed by replacing with `requireAuth()`.\n    20→\n    21→2. **LIKE wildcard metacharacters not escaped in search inputs** (IMPORTANT) — `%` and `_` in search terms acted as SQL LIKE wildcards. Searching for `%` matched every listing. Fixed with `escapeLike()` function and `ESCAPE '\\'` clause on all LIKE conditions.\n    22→\n    23→3. **Mark-all-notifications-read UPDATE unbounded** (IMPORTANT) — Bulk UPDATE with no LIMIT could lock SQLite WAL on large notification sets. Fixed with subquery LIMIT 1000 to bound write duration.\n    24→\n    25→4. **Profile reading history query unbounded** (IMPORTANT) — No LIMIT on readings query. Added LIMIT 200 to prevent memory/bandwidth issues.\n    26→\n    27→5. **ORDER BY via string interpolation** (HARDENING) — Replaced if/else chain with explicit `ORDER_MAP` allowlist for sort parameter. Safe today but fragile pattern.\n    28→\n    29→6. **Nodemailer transporter cached permanently** (IMPORTANT) — Transporter created once and never rebuilt even if SMTP env vars changed. Added config hash comparison to invalidate and rebuild when env vars change.\n    30→\n    31→All 201 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    32→\n    33→## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n    34→- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints (requires full table recreation, manual deletion already handles correctly in listing DELETE handler)\n    35→- `notifications.listing_id` is nullable — CASCADE would delete notifications instead of preserving them with NULL\n    36→- No user deletion feature exists, so CASCADE on user FKs has no practical impact\n    37→\n    38→## Issues Monitored\n    39→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human, ~3 days remaining\n    40→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    41→\n    42→## Next Session Priority\n    43→1. Check production health\n    44→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    45→3. Consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n    46→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"d00998e1-7ba0-47a0-8a36-8978102269d7","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:00:18.978Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-1200.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 12:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 15: found and fixed 6 bugs across search injection, auth bypass, unbounded queries, and transporter caching. Created issue #104, implemented fixes in PR #105, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #104: Code review round 15 (PR #105 — merged + deployed)\n\n6 fixes:\n\n1. **`auto-discord` uses `getSession()` instead of `requireAuth()`** (IMPORTANT) — Bypassed DB user-existence check that all other authenticated endpoints perform. Deleted users with valid cookies could still access this endpoint. Fixed by replacing with `requireAuth()`.\n\n2. **LIKE wildcard metacharacters not escaped in search inputs** (IMPORTANT) — `%` and `_` in search terms acted as SQL LIKE wildcards. Searching for `%` matched every listing. Fixed with `escapeLike()` function and `ESCAPE '\\'` clause on all LIKE conditions.\n\n3. **Mark-all-notifications-read UPDATE unbounded** (IMPORTANT) — Bulk UPDATE with no LIMIT could lock SQLite WAL on large notification sets. Fixed with subquery LIMIT 1000 to bound write duration.\n\n4. **Profile reading history query unbounded** (IMPORTANT) — No LIMIT on readings query. Added LIMIT 200 to prevent memory/bandwidth issues.\n\n5. **ORDER BY via string interpolation** (HARDENING) — Replaced if/else chain with explicit `ORDER_MAP` allowlist for sort parameter. Safe today but fragile pattern.\n\n6. **Nodemailer transporter cached permanently** (IMPORTANT) — Transporter created once and never rebuilt even if SMTP env vars changed. Added config hash comparison to invalidate and rebuild when env vars change.\n\nAll 201 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints (requires full table recreation, manual deletion already handles correctly in listing DELETE handler)\n- `notifications.listing_id` is nullable — CASCADE would delete notifications instead of preserving them with NULL\n- No user deletion feature exists, so CASCADE on user FKs has no practical impact\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human, ~3 days remaining\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":46,"startLine":1,"totalLines":46}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01W2d51o5UMxM4PNLcCzqr4j","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_013tZ4WEztKgnyZ3vKduPEXy","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"feature-dev:code-explorer","description":"Investigate server action errors","prompt":"In the Bookmate Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app, production logs show repeated errors:\n\n```\n[Error: Failed to find Server Action \"x\". This request might be from an older or newer deployment.\n```\n\nThese happen multiple times per hour. Investigate:\n1. Does this app use any Server Actions? Search for \"use server\" directives in the codebase.\n2. Could this be bots/scanners probing the server? \n3. Is there any way to mitigate these errors (e.g., custom error handling for invalid server action IDs)?\n\nJust research and report findings - don't write code."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1779,"cache_read_input_tokens":21771,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1779},"output_tokens":27,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"7c8aa855-af66-4b05-a93d-d0415c67a4dd"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"In the Bookmate Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app, production logs show repeated errors:\n\n```\n[Error: Failed to find Server Action \"x\". This request might be from an older or newer deployment.\n```\n\nThese happen multiple times per hour. Investigate:\n1. Does this app use any Server Actions? Search for \"use server\" directives in the codebase.\n2. Could this be bots/scanners probing the server? \n3. Is there any way to mitigate these errors (e.g., custom error handling for invalid server action IDs)?\n\nJust research and report findings - don't write code."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_013tZ4WEztKgnyZ3vKduPEXy","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"ba53a299-f1f0-4c85-a9c6-8fc3dd5461dc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:02.372Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SMoYBadhYkA2a1BU5LR6uT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     4→\n     5→export default function Error({\n     6→  error,\n     7→  reset,\n     8→}: {\n     9→  error: Error & { digest?: string };\n    10→  reset: () => void;\n    11→}) {\n    12→  return (\n    13→    <div className=\"error-container\">\n    14→      <div\n    15→        style={{\n    16→          fontSize: \"3rem\",\n    17→          marginBottom: \"1rem\",\n    18→        }}\n    19→      >\n    20→        <svg\n    21→          width=\"48\"\n    22→          height=\"48\"\n    23→          viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n    24→          fill=\"none\"\n    25→          stroke=\"var(--color-error)\"\n    26→          strokeWidth={1.5}\n    27→          style={{ margin: \"0 auto\" }}\n    28→        >\n    29→          <circle cx={12} cy={12} r={10} />\n    30→          <line x1={12} y1={8} x2={12} y2={12} />\n    31→          <line x1={12} y1={16} x2={12.01} y2={16} />\n    32→        </svg>\n    33→      </div>\n    34→      <h2>Something went wrong</h2>\n    35→      <p>\n    36→        {error.message || \"An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.\"}\n    37→      </p>\n    38→      <div style={{ display: \"flex\", gap: \"0.75rem\", justifyContent: \"center\" }}>\n    39→        <button onClick={reset} className=\"btn-primary\">\n    40→          Try again\n    41→        </button>\n    42→        <Link href=\"/\" className=\"btn-secondary\" style={{ textDecoration: \"none\" }}>\n    43→          Go home\n    44→        </Link>\n    45→      </div>\n    46→    </div>\n    47→  );\n    48→}\n    49→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_013tZ4WEztKgnyZ3vKduPEXy","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"4fbd4310-0d0a-4eb5-a16a-b371f8a481d5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:14.175Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PtND7cgnN85tcHAc6qra8A","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { NextConfig } from \"next\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→\n     4→const securityHeaders = [\n     5→  {\n     6→    key: \"X-DNS-Prefetch-Control\",\n     7→    value: \"on\",\n     8→  },\n     9→  {\n    10→    key: \"X-Frame-Options\",\n    11→    value: \"SAMEORIGIN\",\n    12→  },\n    13→  {\n    14→    key: \"X-Content-Type-Options\",\n    15→    value: \"nosniff\",\n    16→  },\n    17→  {\n    18→    key: \"Referrer-Policy\",\n    19→    value: \"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\",\n    20→  },\n    21→  {\n    22→    key: \"Permissions-Policy\",\n    23→    value: \"camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()\",\n    24→  },\n    25→  {\n    26→    key: \"X-XSS-Protection\",\n    27→    value: \"1; mode=block\",\n    28→  },\n    29→];\n    30→\n    31→const nextConfig: NextConfig = {\n    32→  outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname),\n    33→  images: {\n    34→    remotePatterns: [\n    35→      {\n    36→        protocol: \"https\",\n    37→        hostname: \"covers.openlibrary.org\",\n    38→      },\n    39→      {\n    40→        protocol: \"https\",\n    41→        hostname: \"archive.org\",\n    42→      },\n    43→      {\n    44→        protocol: \"https\",\n    45→        hostname: \"*.us.archive.org\",\n    46→      },\n    47→    ],\n    48→  },\n    49→  async headers() {\n    50→    return [\n    51→      {\n    52→        source: \"/(.*)\",\n    53→        headers: securityHeaders,\n    54→      },\n    55→    ];\n    56→  },\n    57→};\n    58→\n    59→export default nextConfig;\n    60→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_013tZ4WEztKgnyZ3vKduPEXy","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"84b90a98-add4-4de4-bf38-d9c6f6638ed9","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:14.202Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ACPnAj3RXh7Vf3esZWFtLe","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pending_telegram_chat_id ON pending_telegram_groups(telegram_chat_id);\n   178→`);\n   179→\n   180→export default db;\n   181→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"e93de08d-98fa-464a-9554-846b05aa3d05","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:14.460Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Niy3i2GRAgh82g5idjBNSw","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→import db from \"./db\";\n     4→\n     5→export interface SessionData {\n     6→  userId?: number;\n     7→  email?: string;\n     8→  displayName?: string;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const DEV_SECRET =\n    12→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    13→\n    14→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    15→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    16→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    17→    throw new Error(\n    18→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→  return {\n    22→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    23→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    24→    cookieOptions: {\n    25→      httpOnly: true,\n    26→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    27→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    28→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    29→    },\n    30→  };\n    31→}\n    32→\n    33→export async function getSession() {\n    34→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    35→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→export async function requireAuth() {\n    39→  const session = await getSession();\n    40→  if (!session.userId) {\n    41→    return null;\n    42→  }\n    43→  // Verify the user still exists in the DB — a deleted user's cookie\n    44→  // would otherwise grant full access until the session expires\n    45→  const user = db\n    46→    .prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    47→    .get(session.userId) as { id: number; display_name: string } | undefined;\n    48→  if (!user) {\n    49→    session.destroy();\n    50→    return null;\n    51→  }\n    52→  // Keep session displayName in sync with DB (handles stale cookies)\n    53→  if (user.display_name !== session.displayName) {\n    54→    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    55→    await session.save();\n    56→  }\n    57→  return session;\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"a16f2218-b406-4740-b812-451d4a0d02d8","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:14.463Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GdRsbnFjZqKy93NAqrDrG4","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { NextConfig } from \"next\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→\n     4→const securityHeaders = [\n     5→  {\n     6→    key: \"X-DNS-Prefetch-Control\",\n     7→    value: \"on\",\n     8→  },\n     9→  {\n    10→    key: \"X-Frame-Options\",\n    11→    value: \"SAMEORIGIN\",\n    12→  },\n    13→  {\n    14→    key: \"X-Content-Type-Options\",\n    15→    value: \"nosniff\",\n    16→  },\n    17→  {\n    18→    key: \"Referrer-Policy\",\n    19→    value: \"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\",\n    20→  },\n    21→  {\n    22→    key: \"Permissions-Policy\",\n    23→    value: \"camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()\",\n    24→  },\n    25→  {\n    26→    key: \"X-XSS-Protection\",\n    27→    value: \"1; mode=block\",\n    28→  },\n    29→];\n    30→\n    31→const nextConfig: NextConfig = {\n    32→  outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(__dirname),\n    33→  images: {\n    34→    remotePatterns: [\n    35→      {\n    36→        protocol: \"https\",\n    37→        hostname: \"covers.openlibrary.org\",\n    38→      },\n    39→      {\n    40→        protocol: \"https\",\n    41→        hostname: \"archive.org\",\n    42→      },\n    43→      {\n    44→        protocol: \"https\",\n    45→        hostname: \"*.us.archive.org\",\n    46→      },\n    47→    ],\n    48→  },\n    49→  async headers() {\n    50→    return [\n    51→      {\n    52→        source: \"/(.*)\",\n    53→        headers: securityHeaders,\n    54→      },\n    55→    ];\n    56→  },\n    57→};\n    58→\n    59→export default nextConfig;\n    60→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"4da884dd-0bce-44d6-978d-db52eec315be","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:14.864Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015jUQU6daSJV4eSx1xefjQo","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Check if user has a pending application (e.g., requires_approval was toggled off)\n    57→  const pendingApp = db\n    58→    .prepare(\n    59→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    60→    )\n    61→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    62→\n    63→  if (pendingApp) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"You already have a pending application for this group\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    71→  // Re-read max_group_size inside the transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    72→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    73→    const freshListing = db\n    74→      .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    75→      .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    76→\n    77→    if (!freshListing) {\n    78→      return { error: \"Listing not found\", count: 0, maxSize: 0 };\n    79→    }\n    80→\n    81→    const { count } = db\n    82→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    83→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    84→\n    85→    if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    86→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    db.prepare(\n    90→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    91→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    92→\n    93→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    94→    if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    95→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    96→    }\n    97→\n    98→    return { error: null, count: newCount, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    99→  });\n   100→\n   101→  const result = joinTransaction();\n   102→\n   103→  if (result.error) {\n   104→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n   105→  }\n   106→\n   107→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   108→  // When group just filled, skip new_member notification for the author\n   109→  // since they'll receive the more important group_full notification instead\n   110→  if (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n   111→    notifyGroupFull(\n   112→      listingId,\n   113→      listing.book_title as string,\n   114→      listing.platform_preference as string\n   115→    );\n   116→  } else {\n   117→    notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   118→  }\n   119→\n   120→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n   121→}\n   122→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"7278935b-193b-4ea8-8e5f-37fafc937322","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:18.008Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a6d69ddefde39fa8c","tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":13772,"tool_uses":8,"duration_ms":10201},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"38fe25f1-775c-4916-9f0f-73903b49e53c","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01VmhYAMM3DMsxZ9DK7p8EGL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01TMEe5HtZHeV5Cq22iswHDq","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3754,"cache_read_input_tokens":9764,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3754,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":39,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"03a0221b-2664-4544-8289-f2f1c0d22a9a"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TMEe5HtZHeV5Cq22iswHDq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Re-read max_group_size inside transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    63→      const freshListing = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    66→\n    67→      if (!freshListing) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Listing not found\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Check if group is already full\n    72→      const { count } = db\n    73→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    74→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    75→\n    76→      if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    77→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    78→      }\n    79→\n    80→      // Update application status\n    81→      db.prepare(\n    82→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    83→      ).run(applicationId);\n    84→\n    85→      // Add to members\n    86→      db.prepare(\n    87→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    88→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    89→\n    90→      // Check if group is now full\n    91→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    92→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    93→      if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    94→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    95→\n    96→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    97→        rejectedApps = db\n    98→          .prepare(\n    99→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n   100→          )\n   101→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n   102→\n   103→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n   104→          db.prepare(\n   105→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   106→          ).run(app.id);\n   107→        }\n   108→\n   109→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   110→      }\n   111→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   112→    });\n   113→\n   114→    try {\n   115→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   116→\n   117→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   118→      try {\n   119→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   120→\n   121→        if (result.isFull) {\n   122→          notifyGroupFull(\n   123→            listingId,\n   124→            listing.book_title as string,\n   125→            listing.platform_preference as string\n   126→          );\n   127→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   128→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   129→          }\n   130→        }\n   131→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   132→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   133→      }\n   134→\n   135→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   136→    } catch (err) {\n   137→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   138→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   139→    }\n   140→  } else {\n   141→    // Reject\n   142→    db.prepare(\n   143→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   144→    ).run(applicationId);\n   145→\n   146→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   147→\n   148→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   149→  }\n   150→}\n   151→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"9b2bfbbd-2a7e-4217-aa6c-b2936815e55c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:18.275Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01VmhYAMM3DMsxZ9DK7p8EGL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01LbYdpGa2QFxdmcAutVCymq","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3754,"cache_read_input_tokens":9764,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3754,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":39,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"52805982-46d2-4f65-ae2d-68ae382ded9e"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LbYdpGa2QFxdmcAutVCymq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n    48→  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    49→    // Check if already a member\n    50→    const isMember = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    53→\n    54→    if (isMember) {\n    55→      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    59→    const currentListing = db\n    60→      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    61→      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n    62→\n    63→    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n    64→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    68→    const existingApp = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    71→      )\n    72→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→    if (existingApp) {\n    75→      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    76→        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n    77→      }\n    78→      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    79→        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n    80→      }\n    81→      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    82→      db.prepare(\n    83→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    84→      ).run(existingApp.id);\n    85→\n    86→      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    // Insert new application\n    90→    db.prepare(\n    91→      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    92→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    93→\n    94→    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    95→  });\n    96→\n    97→  const result = applyTransaction();\n    98→\n    99→  if (result.error) {\n   100→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n   101→  }\n   102→\n   103→  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   104→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   105→\n   106→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"d363b3ae-a6ea-458d-a158-d340f248f972","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:18.659Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PuhYChtbGHMpUAhnTdyK6v","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     8→  if (!session) {\n     9→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    10→  }\n    11→\n    12→  // Rate limit: 10 listings per 15 minutes per user\n    13→  const rateLimitKey = `create-listing:${session.userId}`;\n    14→  const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(rateLimitKey, 10);\n    15→  if (!allowed) {\n    16→    return NextResponse.json(\n    17→      { error: \"Too many listings created. Please try again later.\" },\n    18→      { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→\n    22→  try {\n    23→    const body = await req.json();\n    24→    const {\n    25→      bookTitle,\n    26→      bookAuthor,\n    27→      bookCoverUrl,\n    28→      bookOlid,\n    29→      language,\n    30→      readingPace,\n    31→      startDate,\n    32→      meetingFormat,\n    33→      maxGroupSize,\n    34→      requiresApproval,\n    35→      platformPreference,\n    36→    } = body;\n    37→\n    38→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    39→      return NextResponse.json(\n    40→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    41→        { status: 400 }\n    42→      );\n    43→    }\n    44→\n    45→    // Type check all string fields to prevent coercion bypasses\n    46→    if (typeof bookTitle !== \"string\" || typeof bookAuthor !== \"string\" ||\n    47→        typeof readingPace !== \"string\" || typeof startDate !== \"string\" ||\n    48→        typeof meetingFormat !== \"string\") {\n    49→      return NextResponse.json(\n    50→        { error: \"Invalid field types\" },\n    51→        { status: 400 }\n    52→      );\n    53→    }\n    54→\n    55→    if (typeof maxGroupSize !== \"number\" && typeof maxGroupSize !== \"string\") {\n    56→      return NextResponse.json(\n    57→        { error: \"Invalid group size\" },\n    58→        { status: 400 }\n    59→      );\n    60→    }\n    61→\n    62→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    63→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    64→      return NextResponse.json(\n    65→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    66→        { status: 400 }\n    67→      );\n    68→    }\n    69→\n    70→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    71→      return NextResponse.json(\n    72→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    73→        { status: 400 }\n    74→      );\n    75→    }\n    76→\n    77→    if (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n    78→      return NextResponse.json(\n    79→        { error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" },\n    80→        { status: 400 }\n    81→      );\n    82→    }\n    83→\n    84→    const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    85→    if (startDate < today) {\n    86→      return NextResponse.json(\n    87→        { error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" },\n    88→        { status: 400 }\n    89→      );\n    90→    }\n    91→\n    92→    const size = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n    93→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    94→      return NextResponse.json(\n    95→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    96→        { status: 400 }\n    97→      );\n    98→    }\n    99→\n   100→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n   101→      ? platformPreference\n   102→      : \"telegram\";\n   103→\n   104→    // Validate book cover URL — only allow Open Library covers to prevent SSRF/tracking\n   105→    const sanitizedCoverUrl =\n   106→      bookCoverUrl && typeof bookCoverUrl === \"string\" &&\n   107→      bookCoverUrl.startsWith(\"https://covers.openlibrary.org/\")\n   108→        ? bookCoverUrl\n   109→        : \"\";\n   110→\n   111→    // Wrap listing creation + auto-join in a transaction for atomicity\n   112→    const createTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   113→      const result = db\n   114→        .prepare(\n   115→          `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n   116→           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n   117→        )\n   118→        .run(\n   119→          session.userId,\n   120→          bookTitle.trim(),\n   121→          bookAuthor.trim(),\n   122→          sanitizedCoverUrl,\n   123→          bookOlid || \"\",\n   124→          language || \"English\",\n   125→          readingPace.trim(),\n   126→          startDate,\n   127→          meetingFormat,\n   128→          size,\n   129→          requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n   130→          platform\n   131→        );\n   132→\n   133→      // Auto-join the author as a member\n   134→      db.prepare(\n   135→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n   136→      ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n   137→\n   138→      return result.lastInsertRowid;\n   139→    });\n   140→\n   141→    const listingId = createTransaction();\n   142→\n   143→    return NextResponse.json(\n   144→      { id: listingId },\n   145→      { status: 201 }\n   146→    );\n   147→  } catch (error) {\n   148→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n   149→    return NextResponse.json(\n   150→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   151→      { status: 500 }\n   152→    );\n   153→  }\n   154→}\n   155→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"84d22976-280d-4e33-84b7-acc00f1414cd","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:01:19.134Z"}
