{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LpU4r4KjpzfQ4SdcNE547F","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Notification {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  listing_id: number;\n     9→  type: string;\n    10→  message: string;\n    11→  is_read: number;\n    12→  created_at: string;\n    13→  book_title: string;\n    14→}\n    15→\n    16→export default function NotificationsPage() {\n    17→  const [notifications, setNotifications] = useState<Notification[]>([]);\n    18→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    19→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    20→\n    21→  useEffect(() => {\n    22→    fetch(\"/api/notifications\")\n    23→      .then((r) => {\n    24→        if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load notifications\");\n    25→        return r.json();\n    26→      })\n    27→      .then((data) => {\n    28→        setNotifications(data.notifications || []);\n    29→        // Mark all as read (fire-and-forget with silent catch)\n    30→        fetch(\"/api/notifications\", {\n    31→          method: \"PATCH\",\n    32→          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n    34→        }).catch(() => {});\n    35→      })\n    36→      .catch(() => {\n    37→        setError(\"Could not load notifications. Please try again.\");\n    38→      })\n    39→      .finally(() => {\n    40→        setLoading(false);\n    41→      });\n    42→  }, []);\n    43→\n    44→  if (loading) {\n    45→    return (\n    46→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n    47→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n    48→      </div>\n    49→    );\n    50→  }\n    51→\n    52→  if (error) {\n    53→    return (\n    54→      <div className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n    55→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-4 sm:mb-6\">Notifications</h1>\n    56→        <div className=\"text-center py-12\">\n    57→          <p\n    58→            className=\"text-sm mb-4\"\n    59→            style={{ color: \"var(--color-error)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    60→            role=\"alert\""}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"45c4ceca-cbd8-4805-b6a0-76cc7d3cfd7a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:02:01.152Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"ac704d4bc4faa5b2f","tool_use_id":"toolu_013tZ4WEztKgnyZ3vKduPEXy","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Investigate server action errors","usage":{"total_tokens":31070,"tool_uses":22,"duration_ms":78424},"uuid":"a39fd5e7-5ca9-4310-9561-f23218f610ee","session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013tZ4WEztKgnyZ3vKduPEXy","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have everything needed for a thorough analysis. Here is the full report.\n\n---\n\n## Investigation Report: `Failed to find Server Action \"x\"` Errors\n\n### 1. Does this app use Server Actions?\n\n**No.** There is not a single `\"use server\"` directive anywhere in the codebase. Every mutation is handled through conventional Route Handler API endpoints under `/app/api/` (e.g., `/api/auth/login`, `/api/listings/create`, `/api/listings/[id]/join`). There are no `actions.ts` files and no inline server functions. The app uses `\"use client\"` components that call those API routes via `fetch`.\n\nThis means no legitimate user of the application can ever trigger a Server Action call. Every single one of these errors originates from an external source.\n\n### 2. Is this bots/scanners probing the server?\n\n**Yes, with high confidence.** Several factors confirm this:\n\n**The action ID is literally `\"x\"`**\nEvery error in the log is identical: `Failed to find Server Action \"x\"`. A real Next.js Server Action ID is a 40-character hex digest (SHA-1 of the build-time encryption key + action path). The string `\"x\"` is not a plausible legitimate ID from any real application or deployment mismatch — it is a trivial probe value. A scanner that knows the Next.js Server Action protocol is sending `POST` requests with the HTTP header `Next-Action: x` to arbitrary pages and logging what happens.\n\n**Volume and timing pattern**\nThe log shows hits spread across many hours and days, including clustered bursts (e.g., six hits within one minute on 2026-03-22 at 00:00-00:07 UTC, five hits within six minutes at 11:37-11:41 UTC). This is consistent with automated scanning campaigns against internet-exposed Next.js instances.\n\n**The server is directly exposed**\nThe app runs on bare port 3000 at `89.167.127.85:3000` with no reverse proxy, no rate limiting, no IP-based blocking, and `robots.txt` that allows all crawlers to `/`. The server is trivially discoverable and reachable.\n\n**What the scanner is doing mechanically**\nThe `Next-Action` HTTP header is the signal Next.js uses to identify Server Action POST requests. A scanner sends a POST to any page URL with `Next-Action: x` (or similar short strings) in the header. Next.js's router intercepts this before any application code runs, looks up the action ID in its registry, fails to find it, and throws this error — which goes to stderr, which PM2 captures. The application never gets to run any user-visible logic; it just pollutes the error log.\n\n### 3. Mitigation Options\n\nThere are three angles to consider: suppressing the noise, blocking at the infrastructure level, and fixing the underlying log volume.\n\n**A. Pin `NEXT_SERVER_ACTIONS_ENCRYPTION_KEY` (the official fix, but inapplicable here)**\nThe Next.js docs at `https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/failed-to-find-server-action` describe this error as stemming from multi-instance deployments where different servers have different keys. The fix they document — setting `NEXT_SERVER_ACTIONS_ENCRYPTION_KEY` in `.env.local` — only applies to that scenario. Since this app uses zero Server Actions, pinning the key would have no effect on externally injected invalid IDs. This env var is not currently set, and there is no reason to add it.\n\n**B. Block the `Next-Action` header at the Next.js middleware layer**\nBecause the app has no Server Actions, any incoming request carrying a `Next-Action` header is illegitimate by definition. A Next.js middleware file at `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/middleware.ts` could intercept requests with that header and return a `400` before Next.js's internal action dispatcher ever runs and logs the error. This would cleanly eliminate all log noise.\n\n**C. Block at the infrastructure level (most effective)**\nSince the server is directly IP-exposed, a firewall rule (via `ufw` or `iptables`) rate-limiting POST requests or blocking specific scanning IPs would stop the traffic before it ever reaches Node.js. This is the most impactful approach for a bare-metal deployment.\n\n**D. Add a reverse proxy (Nginx/Caddy)**\nPlacing Nginx in front of port 3000 would enable request filtering, rate limiting by IP, and blocking of the `Next-Action` header before requests reach Next.js entirely. This is the standard hardening step for a production self-hosted Next.js app.\n\n**E. Accept the noise but filter log monitoring**\nSince the errors have no functional impact (the app has no Server Actions, so nothing is bypassed or broken), a minimal option is to filter them out at the PM2 log level or in any log aggregation pipeline, so they don't obscure real errors.\n\n### Summary of Key Files\n\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/next.config.ts` — No Server Action configuration; confirms the app does not opt into any Server Action behavior.\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/.env.local` — Only `SESSION_SECRET` is set; `NEXT_SERVER_ACTIONS_ENCRYPTION_KEY` is absent and irrelevant.\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/.env.example` — No mention of `NEXT_SERVER_ACTIONS_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, consistent with the feature never being used.\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/pm2-error-0.log` — 150+ lines of the identical error, all with action ID `\"x\"`, spanning 2026-03-20 through 2026-03-22+.\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/robots.ts` — Blocks `/api/` from crawlers but does not block POST requests and has no effect on scanner tools that ignore robots.txt.\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/layout.tsx` — No `\"use server\"` directives; confirms the whole app is client-fetch-based."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: ac704d4bc4faa5b2f (use SendMessage with to: 'ac704d4bc4faa5b2f' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 31895\ntool_uses: 22\nduration_ms: 78426</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"c2f296c0-1715-4f8a-acef-8e62a69a5f8f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:02:20.798Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"In the Bookmate Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app, production logs show repeated errors:\n\n```\n[Error: Failed to find Server Action \"x\". This request might be from an older or newer deployment.\n```\n\nThese happen multiple times per hour. Investigate:\n1. Does this app use any Server Actions? Search for \"use server\" directives in the codebase.\n2. Could this be bots/scanners probing the server? \n3. Is there any way to mitigate these errors (e.g., custom error handling for invalid server action IDs)?\n\nJust research and report findings - don't write code.","agentId":"ac704d4bc4faa5b2f","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have everything needed for a thorough analysis. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018sx9Gjuwvs6L2MJxb9ozeZ","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"No issues in the PM2 config. Here is the complete, consolidated review:\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Bookmate (Round 17)\n\nReviewed: all files under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/` across the five specified areas. Three confirmed issues meet the confidence threshold.\n\n---\n\n### IMPORTANT — Issue 1: `session.destroy()` is not awaited (stale cookie on deleted user)\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, line 49\n\n`session.destroy()` returns a Promise but is not awaited. The `iron-session` library writes the cleared `Set-Cookie` header asynchronously. Calling it without `await` means the HTTP response is sent before the cookie is cleared, so the client retains the old session cookie. On the next request, the deleted user's cookie still arrives, hits the same `requireAuth()` code path again, finds no DB user again, and again silently fails to clear the cookie. The session is never actually invalidated on the client side until the cookie expires on its own (7 days).\n\nConfidence: 92\n\nFix:\n```ts\nawait session.destroy();\nreturn null;\n```\n\n---\n\n### IMPORTANT — Issue 2: `PATCH /api/notifications` crashes with 500 on missing or non-JSON body\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts`, line 40\n\n`await req.json()` throws a `SyntaxError` if the request body is missing or malformed. There is no try/catch wrapping this call, so the thrown error propagates as an unhandled exception, producing a 500 instead of a structured `{ error: \"...\" }` 400 response. Every other API route in the codebase wraps `req.json()` in a try/catch or has outer catch coverage. This route does not. The production notifications page always sends valid JSON so the happy path is fine, but the contract for external callers (bots, tests, curl) is broken.\n\nConfidence: 85\n\nFix: Wrap the `req.json()` call:\n```ts\nlet body: Record<string, unknown> = {};\ntry {\n  body = await req.json();\n} catch {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid request body\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\nconst { notificationId } = body;\n```\n\n---\n\n### IMPORTANT — Issue 3: `BookSearch` component does not abort in-flight requests on unmount when query is short\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`, lines 47–50\n\nThe `useEffect` on `query` returns a cleanup function that calls `controller.abort()` — but only when the effect body fully executes past the early-return guard (`if (query.length < 3) { setResults([]); return; }`). The early-return path returns `undefined` (no cleanup). If a user types 3+ characters (triggering a fetch), then clears the input (triggering the early return) and the component unmounts while the fetch is still in flight, `abortRef.current` is never aborted. The in-flight fetch then resolves and calls `setResults` on an unmounted component. React 18 silently ignores this, but the dangling fetch and potential state update are a real leak.\n\nConfidence: 83\n\nFix: Abort any pending controller on the early-return path:\n```ts\nif (query.length < 3) {\n  setResults([]);\n  if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n  return;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary of items checked with no findings\n\n- All listing CRUD transactions (join, apply, approve, delete, patch) — correctly use SQLite transactions with inner re-checks\n- `DELETE` TOCTOU guard — correctly re-checks platform links inside the transaction\n- Input validation across all API routes — length limits, type checks, enum validation all present\n- Rate limiting — sliding window implementation is correct; global cleanup interval guard is correct\n- Session cookie security — `httpOnly`, `sameSite: lax`, `maxAge` all set appropriately\n- `next.config.ts` — security headers and image remote patterns look correct\n- `db.ts` — indexes cover all high-frequency foreign key columns; migrations are idempotent\n- `BookSearch` debounce and abort on rapid query changes — correctly cancels prior fetches\n- `notifyGroupFull` being called outside the transaction with pre-captured listing data — correct\n- `profile/reputation` — correctly strips comments from other users' ratings"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a6d69ddefde39fa8c (use SendMessage with to: 'a6d69ddefde39fa8c' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 85918\ntool_uses: 40\nduration_ms: 118691</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"dda1a4a3-babd-40b6-a8e3-fbfd8840030a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:03:06.762Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js application at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, security issues, and code quality problems. The app has already gone through 16 rounds of code review, so focus on areas that may have been missed:\n\n1. **Client-side components** - Check all files in app/components/ and app/app/ for React bugs, state management issues, memory leaks, or incorrect hook usage\n2. **API route edge cases** - Look at all API routes in app/app/api/ for any remaining issues with error handling, validation, or race conditions\n3. **Database schema and queries** - Check app/lib/db.ts for any schema issues, missing indexes, or query problems\n4. **Configuration files** - Check next.config.ts, package.json, ecosystem.config.js for any misconfigurations\n5. **Session/auth edge cases** - Check app/lib/session.ts for any session handling issues\n\nReport only HIGH confidence issues (bugs that would actually manifest in production). Skip cosmetic/style issues. For each finding, rate importance as CRITICAL, IMPORTANT, or HARDENING.","agentId":"a6d69ddefde39fa8c","content":[{"type":"text","text":"No issues in the PM2 config. Here is the complete, consolidated review:\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Bookmate (Round 17)\n\nReviewed: all files under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/` across the five specified areas. Three confirmed issues meet the confidence threshold.\n\n---\n\n### IMPORTANT — Issue 1: `session.destroy()` is not awaited (stale cookie on deleted user)\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, line 49\n\n`session.destroy()` returns a Promise but is not awaited. The `iron-session` library writes the cleared `Set-Cookie` header asynchronously. Calling it without `await` means the HTTP response is sent before the cookie is cleared, so the client retains the old session cookie. On the next request, the deleted user's cookie still arrives, hits the same `requireAuth()` code path again, finds no DB user again, and again silently fails to clear the cookie. The session is never actually invalidated on the client side until the cookie expires on its own (7 days).\n\nConfidence: 92\n\nFix:\n```ts\nawait session.destroy();\nreturn null;\n```\n\n---\n\n### IMPORTANT — Issue 2: `PATCH /api/notifications` crashes with 500 on missing or non-JSON body\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts`, line 40\n\n`await req.json()` throws a `SyntaxError` if the request body is missing or malformed. There is no try/catch wrapping this call, so the thrown error propagates as an unhandled exception, producing a 500 instead of a structured `{ error: \"...\" }` 400 response. Every other API route in the codebase wraps `req.json()` in a try/catch or has outer catch coverage. This route does not. The production notifications page always sends valid JSON so the happy path is fine, but the contract for external callers (bots, tests, curl) is broken.\n\nConfidence: 85\n\nFix: Wrap the `req.json()` call:\n```ts\nlet body: Record<string, unknown> = {};\ntry {\n  body = await req.json();\n} catch {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid request body\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\nconst { notificationId } = body;\n```\n\n---\n\n### IMPORTANT — Issue 3: `BookSearch` component does not abort in-flight requests on unmount when query is short\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`, lines 47–50\n\nThe `useEffect` on `query` returns a cleanup function that calls `controller.abort()` — but only when the effect body fully executes past the early-return guard (`if (query.length < 3) { setResults([]); return; }`). The early-return path returns `undefined` (no cleanup). If a user types 3+ characters (triggering a fetch), then clears the input (triggering the early return) and the component unmounts while the fetch is still in flight, `abortRef.current` is never aborted. The in-flight fetch then resolves and calls `setResults` on an unmounted component. React 18 silently ignores this, but the dangling fetch and potential state update are a real leak.\n\nConfidence: 83\n\nFix: Abort any pending controller on the early-return path:\n```ts\nif (query.length < 3) {\n  setResults([]);\n  if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n  return;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary of items checked with no findings\n\n- All listing CRUD transactions (join, apply, approve, delete, patch) — correctly use SQLite transactions with inner re-checks\n- `DELETE` TOCTOU guard — correctly re-checks platform links inside the transaction\n- Input validation across all API routes — length limits, type checks, enum validation all present\n- Rate limiting — sliding window implementation is correct; global cleanup interval guard is correct\n- Session cookie security — `httpOnly`, `sameSite: lax`, `maxAge` all set appropriately\n- `next.config.ts` — security headers and image remote patterns look correct\n- `db.ts` — indexes cover all high-frequency foreign key columns; migrations are idempotent\n- `BookSearch` debounce and abort on rapid query changes — correctly cancels prior fetches\n- `notifyGroupFull` being called outside the transaction with pre-captured listing data — correct\n- `profile/reputation` — correctly strips comments from other users' ratings"}],"totalDurationMs":118691,"totalTokens":85918,"totalToolUseCount":40,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2301,"cache_read_input_tokens":82549,"output_tokens":1067,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2301},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Bsb7ky1Fh4wVTmqqXeXs8C","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→import db from \"./db\";\n     4→\n     5→export interface SessionData {\n     6→  userId?: number;\n     7→  email?: string;\n     8→  displayName?: string;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const DEV_SECRET =\n    12→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    13→\n    14→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    15→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    16→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    17→    throw new Error(\n    18→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→  return {\n    22→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    23→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    24→    cookieOptions: {\n    25→      httpOnly: true,\n    26→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    27→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    28→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    29→    },\n    30→  };\n    31→}\n    32→\n    33→export async function getSession() {\n    34→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    35→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→export async function requireAuth() {\n    39→  const session = await getSession();\n    40→  if (!session.userId) {\n    41→    return null;\n    42→  }\n    43→  // Verify the user still exists in the DB — a deleted user's cookie\n    44→  // would otherwise grant full access until the session expires\n    45→  const user = db\n    46→    .prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    47→    .get(session.userId) as { id: number; display_name: string } | undefined;\n    48→  if (!user) {\n    49→    session.destroy();\n    50→    return null;\n    51→  }\n    52→  // Keep session displayName in sync with DB (handles stale cookies)\n    53→  if (user.display_name !== session.displayName) {\n    54→    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    55→    await session.save();\n    56→  }\n    57→  return session;\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"9d694c2e-39c5-447f-91a5-50fd1c0b9bef","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:03:13.998Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts","content":"import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\nimport { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\nimport db from \"./db\";\n\nexport interface SessionData {\n  userId?: number;\n  email?: string;\n  displayName?: string;\n}\n\nconst DEV_SECRET =\n  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n\nfunction getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    throw new Error(\n      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    );\n  }\n  return {\n    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    cookieOptions: {\n      httpOnly: true,\n      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n      sameSite: \"lax\",\n      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nexport async function getSession() {\n  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n}\n\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  // Verify the user still exists in the DB — a deleted user's cookie\n  // would otherwise grant full access until the session expires\n  const user = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(session.userId) as { id: number; display_name: string } | undefined;\n  if (!user) {\n    session.destroy();\n    return null;\n  }\n  // Keep session displayName in sync with DB (handles stale cookies)\n  if (user.display_name !== session.displayName) {\n    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    await session.save();\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n","numLines":59,"startLine":1,"totalLines":59}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01X9uqkwsAdvPjSPMXk3PJRJ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET() {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  const notifications = db\n    12→    .prepare(\n    13→      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n    14→       FROM notifications n\n    15→       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n    16→       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n    17→       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n    18→       LIMIT 50`\n    19→    )\n    20→    .all(session.userId);\n    21→\n    22→  const unreadCount = db\n    23→    .prepare(\n    24→      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    25→    )\n    26→    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n    27→\n    28→  return NextResponse.json({\n    29→    notifications,\n    30→    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n    31→  });\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n    35→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    36→  if (!session) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n    41→\n    42→  if (notificationId) {\n    43→    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    44→    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n    45→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→    }\n    47→    db.prepare(\n    48→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n    50→  } else {\n    51→    // Mark all as read — use subquery with LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration\n    52→    db.prepare(\n    53→      `UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1\n    54→       WHERE id IN (\n    55→         SELECT id FROM notifications\n    56→         WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\n    57→         LIMIT 1000\n    58→       )`\n    59→    ).run(session.userId);\n    60→  }\n    61→\n    62→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    63→}\n    64→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"04c1ae21-384a-4860-9a17-316b46a6a06e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:03:14.489Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const notifications = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n       FROM notifications n\n       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n       LIMIT 50`\n    )\n    .all(session.userId);\n\n  const unreadCount = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    )\n    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    notifications,\n    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n\n  if (notificationId) {\n    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n  } else {\n    // Mark all as read — use subquery with LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration\n    db.prepare(\n      `UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1\n       WHERE id IN (\n         SELECT id FROM notifications\n         WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\n         LIMIT 1000\n       )`\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","numLines":64,"startLine":1,"totalLines":64}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017tBYdrjqLNsdvdH48Boe9G","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     5→\n     6→interface BookResult {\n     7→  key: string;\n     8→  title: string;\n     9→  author_name?: string[];\n    10→  cover_i?: number;\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→interface Props {\n    14→  onSelect: (book: {\n    15→    title: string;\n    16→    author: string;\n    17→    coverUrl: string;\n    18→    olid: string;\n    19→  }) => void;\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→export default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n    23→  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    24→  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n    25→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    26→  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n    27→  const [searchError, setSearchError] = useState(false);\n    28→  const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(-1);\n    29→  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    30→  const abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n    31→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    32→  const listboxRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    33→\n    34→  useEffect(() => {\n    35→    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    36→      if (\n    37→        containerRef.current &&\n    38→        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n    39→      ) {\n    40→        setOpen(false);\n    41→      }\n    42→    };\n    43→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    44→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    45→  }, []);\n    46→\n    47→  useEffect(() => {\n    48→    if (query.length < 3) {\n    49→      setResults([]);\n    50→      return;\n    51→    }\n    52→\n    53→    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    54→    if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n    55→\n    56→    const controller = new AbortController();\n    57→    abortRef.current = controller;\n    58→\n    59→    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n    60→      setLoading(true);\n    61→      setSearchError(false);\n    62→      try {\n    63→        const res = await fetch(\n    64→          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`,\n    65→          { signal: controller.signal }\n    66→        );\n    67→        const data = await res.json();\n    68→        setResults(data.docs || []);\n    69→        setOpen(true);\n    70→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n    71→      } catch (err) {\n    72→        if ((err as Error).name !== \"AbortError\") {\n    73→          setResults([]);\n    74→          setSearchError(true);\n    75→        }\n    76→      } finally {\n    77→        if (!controller.signal.aborted) {\n    78→          setLoading(false);\n    79→        }\n    80→      }\n    81→    }, 400);\n    82→\n    83→    return () => {\n    84→      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    85→      controller.abort();\n    86→    };\n    87→  }, [query]);\n    88→\n    89→  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    90→    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    91→    onSelect({\n    92→      title: book.title,\n    93→      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n    94→      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n    95→        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n    96→        : \"\",\n    97→      olid,\n    98→    });\n    99→    setQuery(book.title);\n   100→    setOpen(false);\n   101→    setActiveIndex(-1);\n   102→  };\n   103→\n   104→  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {\n   105→    if (!open || results.length === 0) return;\n   106→\n   107→    switch (e.key) {\n   108→      case \"ArrowDown\":\n   109→        e.preventDefault();\n   110→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev < results.length - 1 ? prev + 1 : 0));\n   111→        break;\n   112→      case \"ArrowUp\":\n   113→        e.preventDefault();\n   114→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev > 0 ? prev - 1 : results.length - 1));\n   115→        break;\n   116→      case \"Enter\":\n   117→        e.preventDefault();\n   118→        if (activeIndex >= 0 && activeIndex < results.length) {\n   119→          handleSelect(results[activeIndex]);\n   120→        }\n   121→        break;\n   122→      case \"Escape\":\n   123→        setOpen(false);\n   124→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n   125→        break;\n   126→    }\n   127→  };\n   128→\n   129→  // Scroll active option into view\n   130→  useEffect(() => {\n   131→    if (activeIndex >= 0 && listboxRef.current) {\n   132→      const activeEl = listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex] as HTMLElement;\n   133→      if (activeEl) {\n   134→        activeEl.scrollIntoView({ block: \"nearest\" });\n   135→      }\n   136→    }\n   137→  }, [activeIndex]);\n   138→\n   139→  const activeDescendant =\n   140→    activeIndex >= 0 && results[activeIndex]\n   141→      ? `book-option-${results[activeIndex].key}`\n   142→      : undefined;\n   143→\n   144→  return (\n   145→    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n   146→      <input\n   147→        type=\"text\"\n   148→        className=\"input-field\"\n   149→        value={query}\n   150→        onChange={(e) => (\n   205→                <Image\n   206→                  src={`https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-S.jpg`}\n   207→                  alt={`Cover of ${book.title}`}\n   208→ 245→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"01de4bc4-87e7-4cf3-bcb3-2aaac9fbe930","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:03:14.956Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx","content":"\"use client\";\n\nimport { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\nimport Image from \"next/image\";\n\ninterface BookResult {\n  key: string;\n  title: string;\n  author_name?: string[];\n  cover_i?: number;\n}\n\ninterface Props {\n  onSelect: (book: {\n    title: string;\n    author: string;\n    coverUrl: string;\n    olid: string;\n  }) => void;\n}\n\nexport default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n  const [searchError, setSearchError] = useState(false);\n  const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(-1);\n  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n  const abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n  const listboxRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n      if (\n        containerRef.current &&\n        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n      ) {\n        setOpen(false);\n      }\n    };\n    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (query.length < 3) {\n      setResults([]);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n\n    const controller = new AbortController();\n    abortRef.current = controller;\n\n    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n      setLoading(true);\n      setSearchError(false);\n      try {\n        const res = await fetch(\n          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`,\n          { signal: controller.signal }\n        );\n        const data = await res.json();\n        setResults(data.docs || []);\n        setOpen(true);\n        setActiveIndex(-1);\n      } catch (err) {\n        if ((err as Error).name !== \"AbortError\") {\n          setResults([]);\n          setSearchError(true);\n        }\n      } finally {\n        if (!controller.signal.aborted) {\n          setLoading(false);\n        }\n      }\n    }, 400);\n\n    return () => {\n      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n      controller.abort();\n    };\n  }, [query]);\n\n  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    onSelect({\n      title: book.title,\n      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n        : \"\",\n      olid,\n    });\n    setQuery(book.title);\n    setOpen(false);\n    setActiveIndex(-1);\n  };\n\n  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {\n    if (!open || results.length === 0) return;\n\n    switch (e.key) {\n      case \"ArrowDown\":\n        e.preventDefault();\n        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev < results.length - 1 ? prev + 1 : 0));\n        break;\n      case \"ArrowUp\":\n        e.preventDefault();\n        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev > 0 ? prev - 1 : results.length - 1));\n        break;\n      case \"Enter\":\n        e.preventDefault();\n        if (activeIndex >= 0 && activeIndex < results.length) {\n          handleSelect(results[activeIndex]);\n        }\n        break;\n      case \"Escape\":\n        setOpen(false);\n        setActiveIndex(-1);\n        break;\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Scroll active option into view\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (activeIndex >= 0 && listboxRef.current) {\n      const activeEl = listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex] as HTMLElement;\n      if (activeEl) {\n        activeEl.scrollIntoView({ block: \"nearest\" });\n      }\n    }\n  }, [activeIndex]);\n\n  const activeDescendant =\n    activeIndex >= 0 && results[activeIndex]\n      ? `book-option-${results[activeIndex].key}`\n      : undefined;\n\n  return (\n    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n      <input\n        type=\"text\"\n        className=\"input-field\"\n        value={query}\n        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}\n        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n        role=\"combobox\"\n        aria-expanded={open && results.length > 0}\n        aria-haspopup=\"listbox\"\n        aria-controls=\"book-search-listbox\"\n        aria-activedescendant={activeDescendant}\n        aria-label=\"Search for a book\"\n        aria-autocomplete=\"list\"\n      />\n      {loading && (\n        <div\n          className=\"absolute right-3 top-3 text-xs\"\n          style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          role=\"status\"\n          aria-live=\"polite\"\n        >\n          Searching...\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {searchError && !loading && (\n        <div\n          className=\"text-xs mt-1\"\n          style={{ color: \"var(--color-error, #dc2626)\" }}\n          role=\"alert\"\n        >\n          Book search is unavailable. Please try again later.\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {open && results.length > 0 && (\n        <div\n          ref={listboxRef}\n          id=\"book-search-listbox\"\n          role=\"listbox\"\n          aria-label=\"Book search results\"\n          className=\"absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 card shadow-lg max-h-72 overflow-y-auto\"\n          style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 0\" }}\n        >\n          {results.map((book, index) => (\n            <div\n              key={book.key}\n              id={`book-option-${book.key}`}\n              role=\"option\"\n              aria-selected={index === activeIndex}\n              onClick={() => handleSelect(book)}\n              className=\"w-full text-left px-4 py-2 hover:bg-gray-50 flex items-center gap-3 cursor-pointer\"\n              style={{\n                backgroundColor: index === activeIndex ? \"rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04)\" : undefined,\n              }}\n            >\n              {book.cover_i ? (\n                <Image\n                  src={`https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-S.jpg`}\n                  alt={`Cover of ${book.title}`}\n                  width={32}\n                  height={48}\n                  className=\"w-8 h-12 object-cover rounded\"\n                />\n              ) : (\n                <div\n                  className=\"w-8 h-12 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm\"\n                  style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\" }}\n                  aria-hidden=\"true\"\n                >\n                  <span role=\"img\" aria-hidden=\"true\">📖</span>\n                </div>\n              )}\n              <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n                <div\n                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold truncate\"\n                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n                >\n                  {book.title}\n                </div>\n                <div\n                  className=\"text-xs truncate\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  {book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\"}\n                </div>\n              </div>\n            </div>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      )}\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n","numLines":245,"startLine":1,"totalLines":245}}}

   session.destroy();\n    return null;","newString":"    await session.destroy();\n    return null;","originalFile":"import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\nimport { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\nimport db from \"./db\";\n\nexport interface SessionData {\n  userId?: number;\n  email?: string;\n  displayName?: string;\n}\n\nconst DEV_SECRET =\n  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n\nfunction getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    throw new Error(\n      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    );\n  }\n  return {\n    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    cookieOptions: {\n      httpOnly: true,\n      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n      sameSite: \"lax\",\n      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nexport async function getSession() {\n  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n}\n\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  // Verify the user still exists in the DB — a deleted user's cookie\n  // would otherwise grant full access until the session expires\n  const user = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(session.userId) as { id: number; display_name: string } | undefined;\n  if (!user) {\n    session.destroy();\n    return null;\n  }\n  // Keep session displayName in sync with DB (handles stale cookies)\n  if (user.display_name !== session.displayName) {\n    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    await session.save();\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":46,"oldLines":7,"newStart":46,"newLines":7,"lines":["     .prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\")","     .get(session.userId) as { (handles stale cookies)"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LYcLUvgGCKxf54HvaxsMsK","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"662050c5-733f-4b96-99a4-bada15fa3d9e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:03:32.812Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts","oldString":"  const { notificationId } = await req.json();","newString":"  let body: Record<string, unknown>;\n  try {\n    body = await req.json();\n  } catch {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid request body\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n  const { notificationId } = body;","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const notifications = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n       FROM notifications n\n       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n       LIMIT 50`\n    )\n    .all(session.userId);\n\n  const unreadCount = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    )\n    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    notifications,\n    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n\n  if (notificationId) {\n    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n  } else {\n    // Mark all as read — use subquery with LIMIT to bound WAL lock duration\n    db.prepare(\n      `UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1\n       WHERE id IN (\n         SELECT id FROM notifications\n         WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\n         LIMIT 1000\n       )`\n    ).run(session.userId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":37,"oldLines":7,"newStart":37,"newLines":13,"lines":["     return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });","   }"," ","-  const { notificationId } = await req.json();","+  let body: Record<string, unknown>;","+  try {","+    body = await req.json();","+  } catch {","+    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid request body\" }, { status: 400 });","+  }","+  const { notificationId } = body;"," ","   if (notificationId) {","     // Validate notificationId is a positive integer"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RFz4Y8cU1nfVfVtgHtqzDX","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"c5726463-4eb1-4c85-808b-6391097a21be","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:03:34.116Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx","oldString":"    if (query.length < 3) {\n      setResults([]);\n      return;\n    }","newString":"    if (query.length < 3) {\n      setResults([]);\n      if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n      return;\n    }","originalFile":"\"use client\";\n\nimport { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\nimport Image from \"next/image\";\n\ninterface BookResult {\n  key: string;\n  title: string;\n  author_name?: string[];\n  cover_i?: number;\n}\n\ninterface Props {\n  onSelect: (book: {\n    title: string;\n    author: string;\n    coverUrl: string;\n    olid: string;\n  }) => void;\n}\n\nexport default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n  const [searchError, setSearchError] = useState(false);\n  const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(-1);\n  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n  const abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n  const listboxRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n      if (\n        containerRef.current &&\n        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n      ) {\n        setOpen(false);\n      }\n    };\n    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (query.length < 3) {\n      setResults([]);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n\n    const controller = new AbortController();\n    abortRef.current = controller;\n\n    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n      setLoading(true);\n      setSearchError(false);\n      try {\n        const res = await fetch(\n          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`,\n          { signal: controller.signal }\n        );\n        const data = await res.json();\n        setResults(data.docs || []);\n        setOpen(true);\n        setActiveIndex(-1);\n      } catch (err) {\n        if ((err as Error).name !== \"AbortError\") {\n          setResults([]);\n          setSearchError(true);\n        }\n      } finally {\n        if (!controller.signal.aborted) {\n          setLoading(false);\n        }\n      }\n    }, 400);\n\n    return () => {\n      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n      controller.abort();\n    };\n  }, [query]);\n\n  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    onSelect({\n      title: book.title,\n      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n        : \"\",\n      olid,\n    });\n    setQuery(book.title);\n    setOpen(false);\n    setActiveIndex(-1);\n  };\n\n  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {\n    if (!open || results.length === 0) return;\n\n    switch (e.key) {\n      case \"ArrowDown\":\n        e.preventDefault();\n        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev < results.length - 1 ? prev + 1 : 0));\n        break;\n      case \"ArrowUp\":\n        e.preventDefault();\n        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev > 0 ? prev - 1 : results.length - 1));\n        break;\n      case \"Enter\":\n        e.preventDefault();\n        if (activeIndex >= 0 && activeIndex < results.length) {\n          handleSelect(results[activeIndex]);\n        }\n        break;\n      case \"Escape\":\n        setOpen(false);\n        setActiveIndex(-1);\n        break;\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Scroll active option into view\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (activeIndex >= 0 && listboxRef.current) {\n      const activeEl = listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex] as HTMLElement;\n      if (activeEl) {\n        activeEl.scrollIntoView({ block: \"nearest\" });\n      }\n    }\n  }, [activeIndex]);\n\n  const activeDescendant =\n    activeIndex >= 0 && results[activeIndex]\n      ? `book-option-${results[activeIndex].key}`\n      : undefined;\n\n  return (\n    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n      <input\n        type=\"text\"\n        className=\"input-field\"\n        value={query}\n        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}\n        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n        role=\"combobox\"\n        aria-expanded={open && results.length > 0}\n        aria-haspopup=\"listbox\"\n        aria-controls=\"book-search-listbox\"\n        aria-activedescendant={activeDescendant}\n        aria-label=\"Search for a book\"\n        aria-autocomplete=\"list\"\n      />\n      {loading && (\n        <div\n          className=\"absolute right-3 top-3 text-xs\"\n          style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          role=\"status\"\n          aria-live=\"polite\"\n        >\n          Searching...\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {searchError && !loading && (\n        <div\n          className=\"text-xs mt-1\"\n          style={{ color: \"var(--color-error, #dc2626)\" }}\n          role=\"alert\"\n        >\n          Book search is unavailable. Please try again later.\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {open && results.length > 0 && (\n        <div\n          ref={listboxRef}\n          id=\"book-search-listbox\"\n          role=\"listbox\"\n          aria-label=\"Book search results\"\n          className=\"absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 card shadow-lg max-h-72 overflow-y-auto\"\n          style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 0\" }}\n        >\n          {results.map((book, index) => (\n            <div\n              key={book.key}\n              id={`book-option-${book.key}`}\n              role=\"option\"\n              aria-selected={index === activeIndex}\n              onClick={() => handleSelect(book)}\n              className=\"w-full text-left px-4 py-2 hover:bg-gray-50 flex items-center gap-3 cursor-pointer\"\n              style={{\n                backgroundColor: index === activeIndex ? \"rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04)\" : undefined,\n              }}\n            >\n              {book.cover_i ? (\n                <Image\n                  src={`https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-S.jpg`}\n                  alt={`Cover of ${book.title}`}\n                  width={32}\n                  height={48}\n                  className=\"w-8 h-12 object-cover rounded\"\n                />\n              ) : (\n                <div\n                  className=\"w-8 h-12 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm\"\n                  style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\" }}\n                  aria-hidden=\"true\"\n                >\n                  <span role=\"img\" aria-hidden=\"true\">📖</span>\n                </div>\n              )}\n              <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n                <div\n                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold truncate\"\n                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n                >\n                  {book.title}\n                </div>\n                <div\n                  className=\"text-xs truncate\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  {book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\"}\n                </div>\n              </div>\n            </div>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      )}\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":47,"oldLines":6,"newStart":47,"newLines":7,"lines":["   useEffect(() => {","     if (query.length < 3) {","       setResults([]);","+      if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();","       return;","     }"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_016vDBGe8TZRHTyR3QChczoy","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014aaMW8FggKQbYcATa4mXuc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/middleware.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n\n/**\n * Middleware to block invalid Server Action requests.\n *\n * This app uses zero Server Actions — all mutations go through API route handlers.\n * Any request with a `Next-Action` header is from a bot/scanner probing for\n * Next.js server actions. Returning 400 here prevents the framework from\n * logging noisy \"Failed to find Server Action\" errors in PM2 logs.\n */\nexport function middleware(req: NextRequest) {\n  if (req.headers.get(\"next-action\")) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Server Actions are not supported\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.next();\n}\n\nexport const config = {\n  // Run on all routes except static assets and internal Next.js paths\n  matcher: [\"/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)\"],\n};\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":658,"cache_read_input_tokens":32593,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":658},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"0dfb4fe9-0e9a-4227-82aa-47b7649e6c79"}

Returning 400 here prevents the framework from\n * logging noisy \"Failed to find Server Action\" errors in PM2 logs.\n */\nexport function middleware(req: NextRequest) {\n  if (req.headers.get(\"next-action\")) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Server Actions are not supported\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.next();\n}\n\nexport const config = {\n  // Run on all routes except static assets and internal Next.js paths\n  matcher: [\"/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)\"],\n};\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

189→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"a0685b6f-c0be-4900-841c-d2866a4eb089","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:04:20.736Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/__tests__/api/notifications/notifications.test.ts","content":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      listing_id INTEGER,\n      type TEXT NOT NULL,\n      message TEXT NOT NULL,\n      is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockRequireAuth = vi.hoisted(() => vi.fn());\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/session\", () => ({\n  requireAuth: mockRequireAuth,\n}));\n\nconst { GET, PATCH } = await import(\"@/app/api/notifications/route\");\n\ndescribe(\"GET /api/notifications\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, \"user@test.com\", \"hash\", \"Test User\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listings (id, author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"Test Book\", \"Test Author\", \"1 chapter/week\", \"2026-04-01\", \"text\", 4);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 401 when not authenticated\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    const response = await GET();\n    expect(response.status).toBe(401);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns empty notifications for new user\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n    const response = await GET();\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.notifications).toEqual([]);\n    expect(data.unreadCount).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns notifications with unread count\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"member_joined\", \"Someone joined your group\", 0);\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"group_full\", \"Your group is now full\", 1);\n\n    const response = await GET();\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.notifications).toHaveLength(2);\n    expect(data.unreadCount).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"includes book_title from listing join\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"member_joined\", \"Someone joined\");\n\n    const response = await GET();\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.notifications[0].book_title).toBe(\"Test Book\");\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe(\"PATCH /api/notifications\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, \"user@test.com\", \"hash\", \"Test User\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listings (id, author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"Test Book\", \"Test Author\", \"1 chapter/week\", \"2026-04-01\", \"text\", 4);\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (id, user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, 1, \"member_joined\", \"Someone joined\", 0);\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (id, user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(2, 1, 1, \"group_full\", \"Group is full\", 0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 401 when not authenticated\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/notifications\", {\n      method: \"PATCH\",\n      body: { notificationId: 1 },\n    });\n    const response = await PATCH(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(401);\n  });\n\n  it(\"marks a single notification as read\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/notifications\", {\n      method: \"PATCH\",\n      body: { notificationId: 1 },\n    });\n    const response = await PATCH(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.ok).toBe(true);\n\n    // Verify only one was marked as read\n    const unread = testDb.prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = 1 AND is_read = 0\"\n    ).get() as { count: number };\n    expect(unread.count).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"marks all notifications as read when no notificationId\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/notifications\", {\n      method: \"PATCH\",\n      body: {},\n    });\n    const response = await PATCH(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n\n    const unread = testDb.prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = 1 AND is_read = 0\"\n    ).get() as { count: number };\n    expect(unread.count).toBe(0);\n  });\n});\n","numLines":189,"startLine":1,"totalLines":189}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017Joc8VEP2ydVYU8JQCwaPY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest } from \"next/server\";\n     2→\n     3→/**\n     4→ * Create a NextRequest for testing API routes.\n     5→ */\n     6→export function createTestRequest(\n     7→  url: string,\n     8→  options: {\n     9→    method?: string;\n    10→    body?: Record<string, unknown>;\n    11→    headers?: Record<string, string>;\n    12→  } = {}\n    13→) {\n    14→  const { method = \"GET\", body, headers = {} } = options;\n    15→\n    16→  const init: RequestInit = {\n    17→    method,\n    18→    headers: {\n    19→      \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    20→      ...headers,\n    21→    },\n    22→  };\n    23→\n    24→  if (body) {\n    25→    init.body = JSON.stringify(body);\n    26→  }\n    27→\n    28→  return new NextRequest(new URL(url, \"http://localhost:3000\"), init as NextRequest);\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→/**\n    32→ * Parse the JSON body from a NextResponse.\n    33→ */\n    34→export async function parseResponse<T = Record<string, unknown>>(\n    35→  response: Response\n    36→): Promise<{ status: number; data: T }> {\n    37→  const data = (await response.json()) as T;\n    38→  return { status: response.status, data };\n    39→}\n    40→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"5922fa3c-0893-46ae-afcd-1f08bb41c301","timestamp":"2026-03-24T14:04:25.492Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/__tests__/helpers/mock-request.ts","content":"import { NextRequest } from \"next/server\";\n\n/**\n * Create a NextRequest for testing API routes.\n */\nexport function createTestRequest(\n  url: string,\n  options: {\n    method?: string;\n    body?: Record<string, unknown>;\n    headers?: Record<string, string>;\n  } = {}\n) {\n  const { method = \"GET\", body, headers = {} } = options;\n\n  const init: RequestInit = {\n    method,\n    headers: {\n      \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n      ...headers,\n    },\n  };\n\n  if (body) {\n    init.body = JSON.stringify(body);\n  }\n\n  return new NextRequest(new URL(url, \"http://localhost:3000\"), init as NextRequest);\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse the JSON body from a NextResponse.\n */\nexport async function parseResponse<T = Record<string, unknown>>(\n  response: Response\n): Promise<{ status: number; data: T }> {\n  const data = (await response.json()) as T;\n  return { status: response.status, data };\n}\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}

 it(\"marks all notifications as read when no notificationId\", async () => {","newString":"  it(\"returns 400 for invalid/missing JSON body\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ \"http://localhost:3000\"),\n      { method: \"PATCH\", headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" } }\n    );\n    const response = await PATCH(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.error).toBe(\"Invalid request body\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"marks all notifications as read when no notificationId\", async () => {","originalFile":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      listing_id INTEGER,\n      type TEXT NOT NULL,\n      message TEXT NOT NULL,\n      is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockRequireAuth = vi.hoisted(() => vi.fn());\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/session\", () => ({\n  requireAuth: mockRequireAuth,\n}));\n\nconst { GET, PATCH } = await import(\"@/app/api/notifications/route\");\n\ndescribe(\"GET /api/notifications\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, \"user@test.com\", \"hash\", \"Test User\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listings (id, author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"Test Book\", \"Test Author\", \"1 chapter/week\", \"2026-04-01\", \"text\", 4);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 401 when not authenticated\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    const response = await GET();\n    expect(response.status).toBe(401);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns empty notifications for new user\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n    const response = await GET();\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.notifications).toEqual([]);\n    expect(data.unreadCount).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns notifications with unread count\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"member_joined\", \"Someone joined your group\", 0);\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"group_full\", \"Your group is now full\", 1);\n\n    const response = await GET();\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.notifications).toHaveLength(2);\n    expect(data.unreadCount).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"includes book_title from listing join\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"member_joined\", \"Someone joined\");\n\n    const response = await GET();\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.notifications[0].book_title).toBe(\"Test Book\");\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe(\"PATCH /api/notifications\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM notifications\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, \"user@test.com\", \"hash\", \"Test User\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listings (id, author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, \"Test Book\", \"Test Author\", \"1 chapter/week\", \"2026-04-01\", \"text\", 4);\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (id, user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, 1, 1, \"member_joined\", \"Someone joined\", 0);\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO notifications (id, user_id, listing_id, type, message, is_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(2, 1, 1, \"group_full\", \"Group is full\", 0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 401 when not authenticated\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/notifications\", {\n      method: \"PATCH\",\n      body: { notificationId: 1 },\n    });\n    const response = await PATCH(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(401);\n  });\n\n  it(\"marks a single notification as read\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/notifications\", {\n      method: \"PATCH\",\n      body: { notificationId: 1 },\n    });\n    const response = await PATCH(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.ok).toBe(true);\n\n    // Verify only one was marked as read\n    const unread = testDb.prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = 1 AND is_read = 0\"\n    ).get() as { count: number };\n    expect(unread.count).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"marks all notifications as read when no notificationId\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue({ userId: 1 });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/notifications\", {\n      method: \"PATCH\",\n      body: {},\n    });\n    const response = await PATCH(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(200);\n\n    const unread = testDb.prepare(\n      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = 1 AND is_read = 0\"\n    ).get() as { count: number };\n    expect(unread.count).toBe(0);\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":170,"oldLines":6,"newStart":170,"newLines":20,"lines":["     expect(unread.count).toBe(1);"," {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01BY5Job2RAiLu8y3MgKJajV","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01WyqdgmSucww5JcAm8MABgu","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create \\\n  --title \"Code review round 17: session destroy, PATCH body, abort leak, server action probes\" \\\n  --label \"bug,approved\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Findings\n\n1. **`session.destroy()` not awaited** (IMPORTANT) — Deleted user's cookie never actually cleared on client side because `destroy()` returns a Promise that wasn't awaited.\n\n2. **PATCH /api/notifications crashes on missing body** (IMPORTANT) — `req.json()` throws unhandled SyntaxError when body is missing/malformed, producing 500 instead of 400.\n\n3. **BookSearch abort leak on early return** (IMPORTANT) — In-flight fetch not aborted when query shortened below 3 chars, causing potential state update on unmounted component.\n\n4. **Server Action probe noise in PM2 logs** (HARDENING) — Bots sending `Next-Action: x` header cause repeated \"Failed to find Server Action\" errors. Added middleware to return 400 since this app uses zero Server Actions.\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Create GitHub issue"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":140,"cache_read_input_tokens":38398,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":140},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"df8e3316-4a3d-4661-adeb-90ad5384c19b","uuid":"6f12a69a-a524-47ba-a496-4e11ce858e1f"}
